body:
TCA terminate self-kill fix. The current approach fails because the session can't kill itself mid-execution cleanly. Fix as follows:

CORRECT APPROACH — deferred self-terminate via scheduled task:
1. When TCA terminate is received, do all the safe work first while still alive:
   - Detach any EA from MT4 charts (if MT4 running)
   - Close MetaEditor (if running)
   - Close MT4 (if running)
   - Send final relay message: TCA TERMINATED [timestamp] — all processes closed, session closing in 5s
   - Write a one-shot self-destruct task to Windows Task Scheduler:
      = New-ScheduledTaskAction -Execute 'C:\Program Files\PowerShell\pwsh.exe' -Argument '-NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -WindowStyle Hidden -Command "Start-Sleep 5; Get-Process node,pwsh | Where-Object {.Id -ne } | Stop-Process -Force"'
      = New-ScheduledTaskTrigger -Once -At (Get-Date).AddSeconds(3)
     Register-ScheduledTask -TaskName 'TCA-SelfDestruct' -Action  -Trigger  -Force
   - Then exit the Claude session normally with /exit or exit 0
   The scheduled task fires 3-5s later and cleans up any lingering node/pwsh processes that didn't exit cleanly.

2. For TCA sleep: same sequence, but the self-destruct task also triggers Shift+Alt+0 / SetSuspendState after killing processes.

3. After registering the self-destruct task, unregister it automatically on next TCA wake so stale tasks don't accumulate:
   Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName 'TCA-SelfDestruct' -Confirm: -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

Update tca-commands skill with this approach. Test terminate now and confirm the session actually closes.
